301.name: Mom60 + Top-1 Step (cc3_302.py)
301.description: A 3-state momentum rotator that combines a 200-bar price median trend filter with a 60-day return signal on QQQ to graduate TQQQ exposure. In bull mode both signals agree and the portfolio is fully in leveraged tech; in mixed mode it splits between TQQQ and the largest-cap stock from the universe; in bear mode it moves entirely into the top market-cap stock as a defensive equity. The universe is selected as the top 100 stocks by dollar volume, then narrowed to the single largest by market cap.
301.overfit_notes: Two standard momentum components: a 200-bar historical median as a price-level trend gate and a 60-day return crossover. The 3-state step allocation (1.0 / 0.5+0.5 / 0.0+1.0) and the choice of TQQQ as the risk asset are the main sources of overfitting. Overall fit is mild.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: Trend gate AND 60-day return positive → 100% TQQQ (BULL)

rules.label: Entry (mixed)
rules.text: Exactly one signal true → 50% TQQQ + 50% Top-1 stock (MIXED)

rules.label: Entry (bear)
rules.text: Both signals false → 100% Top-1 largest-cap stock (BEAR)

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / largest market-cap stock from top-100 dollar-volume universe

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

302.name: Quad-Mom 5-state (cc3_303.py)
302.description: A 5-state trend-following rotator that scores QQQ across four momentum lookbacks — 5-day, 20-day, 60-day, and 120-day — and blends TQQQ and cash proportionally to how many signals are bullish. With all four lookbacks positive the portfolio is fully in TQQQ; with none it is fully in cash via BIL. The allocation steps down by 25 percentage points for each bullish signal lost.
302.overfit_notes: Four lookbacks (5, 20, 60, 120 days) are individually tuned; their combination creates 5 discrete allocation levels (0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100% TQQQ). The choice of TQQQ and BIL as the only vehicles adds further selection bias.

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of positive returns over 5, 20, 60, 120 days; TQQQ weight = n×25%, BIL weight = (4−n)×25%

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

303.name: Mom20+60+Med+Top1 (cc3_304.py)
303.description: A 4-state hybrid rotator that counts three independent bullish conditions on QQQ — a 200-bar price median trend gate, a 20-day return, and a 60-day return — and maps the count to allocations across TQQQ, the largest-cap stock, and cash. Full agreement produces 100% TQQQ; partial agreement blends in the top market-cap stock; no agreement moves to cash via BIL. The universe selection targets the top dollar-volume stocks, narrowed to a single large-cap holding.
303.overfit_notes: Three momentum inputs (median gate, 20-day, 60-day) with non-standard allocation weights at n=3 (1.0/0.0/0.0), n=2 (0.7/0.3/0.0), n=1 (0.0/0.5/0.5), and n=0 (0.0/0.0/1.0). The 0.7/0.3 split at n=2 is custom-tuned.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 20-day positive return, 60-day positive return]; n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 70% TQQQ+30% Top-1; n=1 → 50% Top-1+50% BIL; n=0 → 100% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / largest market-cap stock / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

304.name: Mom252 + Median + Top-1 (cc3_305.py)
304.description: A 3-state momentum rotator that combines a 200-bar rolling median trend filter with a full 252-day (annual) return signal on QQQ to set TQQQ exposure. When both signals agree the portfolio is fully in TQQQ; when they disagree it splits 50/50 between TQQQ and the largest-cap stock; when both are bearish it holds only the top market-cap stock as a defensive equity position. Uses a longer annual momentum window compared to similar strategies in this series.
304.overfit_notes: Two textbook momentum inputs: a 200-bar median trend gate and a 252-day return crossover. The 3-state step allocation and TQQQ selection are mild sources of fit; the annual lookback is a standard setting.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > median of last 200 closes

rules.label: Entry (trend)
rules.text: Trend gate AND 252-day return positive → 100% TQQQ

rules.label: Entry (mixed)
rules.text: Exactly one signal true → 50% TQQQ + 50% Top-1 stock

rules.label: Entry (bear)
rules.text: Both signals false → 100% Top-1 largest-cap stock

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / largest market-cap stock from top-100 dollar-volume universe

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

305.name: Mom 20+60+120 + Top-3 (cc3_306.py)
305.description: A 5-state hybrid rotator that counts four bullish conditions on QQQ — a 200-bar median gate plus 20-, 60-, and 120-day returns — and maps the count to allocations across TQQQ, an equal-weight basket of the top-3 market-cap stocks, and cash. Full agreement leads to 100% TQQQ; with no signal confirming, the portfolio goes entirely to cash. The top-3 stock basket is used as an intermediate risk-off position rather than a single mega-cap stock.
305.overfit_notes: Four signals (median gate, 20-, 60-, 120-day returns) and a 5-level allocation table. The all-cash plan at n=0 is distinct from sibling strategies. Top-3 universe selection by market cap within the top-100 dollar-volume stocks adds selection tuning.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 20-day return, 60-day return, 120-day return]; n=4 → 100% TQQQ; n=3 → 50% TQQQ+50% Top-3; n=2 → 100% Top-3; n=1 → 100% Top-3; n=0 → 100% cash (all liquidated)

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / top-3 market-cap stocks (equal weight) / cash

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

306.name: Mom20+ADX+Med 4-state (cc3_307.py)
306.description: A 4-state momentum-strength hybrid that combines a 200-bar price median trend gate, a 20-day return, and the directional bias of ADX on QQQ to graduate TQQQ vs. cash allocation. Each bullish signal adds one step of TQQQ exposure, with a custom allocation table mapping 0–3 bullish signals to 0–100% TQQQ. The ADX component uses the directional index comparison rather than an absolute level threshold.
306.overfit_notes: Three inputs (median gate, 20-day return, ADX +DI > -DI) with a tuned 4-level allocation: (1.0, 0.0), (0.7, 0.3), (0.3, 0.7), (0.0, 1.0). The 0.7/0.3 and 0.3/0.7 splits are non-standard.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: ADX(14) positive directional index > negative directional index

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 20-day return, ADX bullish]; n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 70% TQQQ+30% BIL; n=1 → 30% TQQQ+70% BIL; n=0 → 100% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

307.name: Mom20+W%R+Med 4-state (cc3_308.py)
307.description: A 4-state momentum hybrid that uses a 200-bar price median trend gate, a 20-day return, and Williams %R on QQQ to step TQQQ and cash allocation. The Williams %R component uses the midline as its threshold, treating values above -50 as bullish. The 4-state allocation table is identical to the ADX sibling in this batch.
307.overfit_notes: Three inputs: median gate, 20-day return, and Williams %R(14) > -50. The -50 midline threshold is canonical for W%R. The 0.7/0.3 and 0.3/0.7 allocation splits are custom-tuned.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Williams %R(14) > -50

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 20-day return, W%R bullish]; n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 70% TQQQ+30% BIL; n=1 → 30% TQQQ+70% BIL; n=0 → 100% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

308.name: Mom60+MFI+Med 4-state (cc3_309.py)
308.description: A 4-state momentum-volume hybrid that uses a 200-bar price median trend gate, a 60-day return, and Money Flow Index on QQQ to graduate TQQQ vs. cash allocation. The MFI component uses the 50-level midline as its bull/bear signal. This variant uses a longer 60-day momentum lookback compared to the 20-day siblings.
308.overfit_notes: Three inputs: median gate, 60-day return, and MFI(14) > 50. The 50 midline for MFI is canonical. The 0.7/0.3 and 0.3/0.7 allocation splits are custom-tuned; choosing 60 rather than 20 as the momentum window is a tuned parameter.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 60-day return, MFI bullish]; n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 70% TQQQ+30% BIL; n=1 → 30% TQQQ+70% BIL; n=0 → 100% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

309.name: Mom20+OBV+Med 4-state (cc3_310.py)
309.description: A 4-state momentum-volume hybrid that pairs a 200-bar price median trend gate and a 20-day return with a custom OBV slope signal on QQQ. On-balance volume is accumulated over 200 bars and a linear regression slope over the last 30 OBV values is computed manually; a positive slope is treated as bullish. The allocation table steps TQQQ and BIL in the same 4-level pattern as the ADX and W%R siblings.
309.overfit_notes: Three inputs: median gate, 20-day return, and a hand-computed OBV linear slope over 30 bars. The 30-bar OBV regression window and the sign-only slope condition are both tuned choices. Allocation splits of 0.7/0.3 and 0.3/0.7 are custom.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: OBV linear regression slope over last 30 bars > 0

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 20-day return, OBV slope positive]; n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 70% TQQQ+30% BIL; n=1 → 30% TQQQ+70% BIL; n=0 → 100% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

310.name: Mom20+Sto+Med+Top1 (cc3_311.py)
310.description: A 4-state hybrid rotator that uses a 200-bar price median gate, a 20-day return, and Stochastic %K on QQQ to step exposures across TQQQ, the largest market-cap stock, and cash. Full bull agreement goes to 100% TQQQ; partial agreement blends in the top-1 large-cap stock; no signal goes to a mix of the top-1 stock and BIL. The Stochastic uses the 50-level midline as its bull/bear threshold.
310.overfit_notes: Three inputs: median gate, 20-day return, and Stochastic %K(14,3,3) > 50. The plan at n=1 (0.0/1.0/0.0) and n=0 (0.0/0.5/0.5) redirect risk to a stock rather than to BIL, which is a custom design choice.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Stochastic %K(14,3,3) > 50

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 20-day return, Stochastic bullish]; n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 50% TQQQ+50% Top-1; n=1 → 100% Top-1; n=0 → 50% Top-1+50% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / largest market-cap stock / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

311.name: Mom20+OBV+ADX+Med 5-state (cc3_312.py)
311.description: A 5-state momentum rotator that scores four independent signals on QQQ — a 200-bar median gate, a 20-day return, ADX directional bias, and OBV regression slope — and maps the count to a proportional TQQQ vs. BIL allocation. Each additional bullish signal adds 25% to TQQQ allocation. The OBV slope is computed via linear regression over 30 bars of manually accumulated on-balance volume.
311.overfit_notes: Four inputs: median gate, 20-day return, ADX +DI > -DI, and OBV slope > 0 over 30 bars. The 30-bar OBV regression window and the 5-level step table (0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%) are tuned design choices.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: ADX(14) +DI > -DI AND OBV linear regression slope (30 bars) > 0

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 20-day return, ADX bullish, OBV slope positive]; TQQQ weight = n×25%, BIL weight = (4−n)×25%

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

312.name: Mom20+W%R+OBV+Med 5-state (cc3_313.py)
312.description: A 5-state momentum rotator that scores four signals on QQQ — a 200-bar median gate, a 20-day return, Williams %R, and OBV regression slope — and maps the sum to a proportional TQQQ vs. BIL step allocation. It replaces the ADX component from the sibling strategy with Williams %R at the -50 midline threshold. The OBV slope is computed via linear regression over 30 bars.
312.overfit_notes: Four inputs: median gate, 20-day return, W%R(14) > -50, and OBV slope (30-bar regression) > 0. Canonical W%R threshold of -50; the 5-step allocation and 30-bar OBV window are tuned.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Williams %R(14) > -50 AND OBV linear regression slope (30 bars) > 0

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 20-day return, W%R bullish, OBV slope positive]; TQQQ weight = n×25%, BIL weight = (4−n)×25%

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

313.name: Mom20+OBV+Top-1 Step (cc3_314.py)
313.description: A 4-state hybrid rotator that combines a 200-bar price median gate, a 20-day return, and an OBV regression slope on QQQ to allocate between TQQQ, the largest-cap stock, and a partial large-cap position. Rather than routing risk-off exposure to cash, the bear state parks 50% in the top market-cap stock. The OBV slope is derived from a 30-bar linear regression over manually computed on-balance volume.
313.overfit_notes: Three inputs: median gate, 20-day return, and 30-bar OBV regression slope > 0. The bear-state allocation (0.0/0.5) routing to equity rather than cash is a custom design choice.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: OBV linear regression slope (30 bars) > 0

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 20-day return, OBV slope positive]; n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 50% TQQQ+50% Top-1; n=1 → 100% Top-1; n=0 → 50% Top-1

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / largest market-cap stock from top-100 dollar-volume universe

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

314.name: Mom20+MFI+OBV+Med 5-state (cc3_315.py)
314.description: A 5-state momentum-volume rotator that counts four independent bullish signals on QQQ — a 200-bar median gate, a 20-day return, Money Flow Index above 50, and a positive OBV regression slope — and steps TQQQ vs. BIL allocation in 25% increments. The OBV component uses a manually computed linear regression over 30 bars. This is the most comprehensive single-instrument signal stack in this batch.
314.overfit_notes: Four inputs: median gate, 20-day return, MFI(14) > 50, and OBV slope (30-bar regression) > 0. The 30-bar OBV window and the 5-level step table are tuned; the MFI and median thresholds are canonical.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: MFI(14) > 50 AND OBV linear regression slope (30 bars) > 0

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 20-day return, MFI bullish, OBV slope positive]; TQQQ weight = n×25%, BIL weight = (4−n)×25%

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

315.name: Mom60+MFI+Top-1 Step (cc3_316.py)
315.description: A 4-state hybrid rotator that combines a 200-bar price median gate, a 60-day return, and Money Flow Index on QQQ to allocate between TQQQ, the largest-cap stock, and cash. The bear state blends the top market-cap stock with BIL rather than going fully to cash. Uses a longer 60-day momentum lookback relative to the 20-day siblings.
315.overfit_notes: Three inputs: median gate, 60-day return, and MFI(14) > 50. The n=1 plan (0.0/1.0/0.0 — all in top-1 stock) and n=0 plan (0.0/0.5/0.5) redirect risk to equity; these are custom allocation choices. The 60-day lookback is a tuned variant.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 60-day return, MFI bullish]; n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 50% TQQQ+50% Top-1; n=1 → 100% Top-1; n=0 → 50% Top-1+50% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / largest market-cap stock / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

It uses a shorter 15-day momentum window compared to the 20-day siblings in the prior batch. The OBV slope is derived from a 30-bar linear regression over manually accumulated on-balance volume.
316.overfit_notes: Four inputs: median gate, 15-day return (non-standard lookback), MFI(14) > 50, and OBV slope (30 bars) > 0. The 15-day lookback is a fine-tuned variant of the standard 20-day; the 30-bar OBV regression window is also custom.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Strength filter

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 15-day return, MFI bullish, OBV slope positive]; TQQQ weight = n×25%, BIL weight = (4−n)×25%

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

317.name: Mom20+MFI+OBV+Top1 5-state (cc3_318.py)
317.description: A 5-state hybrid rotator that counts four signals on QQQ — a 200-bar median gate, a 20-day return, MFI, and OBV slope — and maps the score to allocations across TQQQ, the largest-cap stock, and BIL. Unlike the BIL-only siblings, the bear state mixes the top market-cap stock with cash, and the intermediate states blend TQQQ with the top-1 large cap. The OBV slope is a 30-bar linear regression.
317.overfit_notes: Four inputs combined with a 5-state allocation table that uses non-standard weights: n=4 (1.0/0/0), n=3 (0.65/0.35/0), n=2 (0.3/0.7/0), n=1 (0/0.7/0.3), n=0 (0/0.3/0.7). The 0.65/0.35 and 0.3/0.7 blends are custom-tuned.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: MFI(14) > 50 AND OBV linear regression slope (30 bars) > 0

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 20-day return, MFI bullish, OBV slope positive]; n=4 → 100% TQQQ; n=3 → 65% TQQQ+35% Top-1; n=2 → 30% TQQQ+70% Top-1; n=1 → 70% Top-1+30% BIL; n=0 → 30% Top-1+70% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / largest market-cap stock / BIL

rules.label: Trend gate

319.overfit_notes: Same 5-state structure with a 60-day momentum lookback. Non-standard allocation weights across all 5 levels are custom-tuned. The 60-day window is a variant of the standard 20-day.

rules.label: Trend gate

The 10-day window makes this the most reactive momentum variant in the batch-64 series. Allocation follows the same graduated blend of TQQQ, the largest-cap stock, and BIL used across the batch.
320.overfit_notes: Same 5-state structure with a 10-day momentum lookback — the shortest in the series and a fine-tuned parameter. Non-standard allocation weights across all 5 levels (0.65/0.35, 0.3/0.7, 0.7/0.3, 0.3/0.7) are custom.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

321.description: A 4-state hybrid rotator that counts three signals on QQQ — a 200-bar median trend gate, a 20-day return, and MFI(14) — and steps allocation across TQQQ, an equal-weight basket of the top-3 market-cap stocks, and BIL. The top-3 stock basket serves as an intermediate risk-off position between leveraged tech and cash. In the bear state the portfolio splits between the stock basket and BIL.
321.overfit_notes: Three inputs: median gate, 20-day return, MFI(14) > 50. The n=1 plan routes entirely to the stock basket (not cash), and n=0 splits between stocks and BIL; these custom allocations distinguish it from BIL-only siblings.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Entry

344.description: A 4-state hybrid rotator using a 200-bar median gate, a 20-day return, and Stochastic %K(14,3,3) above 50 on QQQ, with a single largest-cap stock as the intermediate risk-off vehicle. The Stochastic midline at 50 is the standard bullish/bearish threshold. This is the most concentrated universe variant in the Stochastic series of this batch.
344.overfit_notes: Three inputs: median gate, 20-day return, Stochastic %K(14,3,3) > 50. The top-1 universe concentration is the custom design choice; the Stochastic threshold and 4-state allocation are consistent with the series.

rules.label: Trend gate
rules.text: QQQ price > 200-bar historical median

rules.label: Strength filter
rules.text: Stochastic %K(14,3,3) > 50

rules.label: Entry
rules.text: n = count of: [trend gate, 20-day return, Stochastic bullish]; n=3 → 100% TQQQ; n=2 → 50% TQQQ+50% Top-1; n=1 → 100% Top-1; n=0 → 50% Top-1+50% BIL

rules.label: Symbols
rules.text: Signal: QQQ. Execution: TQQQ / largest market-cap stock / BIL

rules.label: Rebalance
rules.text: Daily, 30 min after market open; trades only on state change

350.name: Mom40+MFI+Top3 (cc3_351.py)
350.description: A 4-state hybrid rotator using a 200-bar median gate, a 40-day return, and MFI(14) on QQQ to step allocation across TQQQ, the equal-weight top-3 market-cap stock basket, and BIL. The 40-day momentum lookback sits between the 30-day and 60-day variants, providing an intermediate reaction speed. The allocation table and universe match the Top-3 MFI siblings from batches 65-71.
350.overfit_notes: Three inputs: median gate, 40-day return (a fine-tuned non-standard lookback between 30 and 60), MFI(14) > 50. The 40-day window is a custom parameter not seen in sibling strategies.
